description: Multi-Family Residential (Group R-2) submittal checklist for the City of Santa Rosa, based on 2022 California Building Code cycle.

items.description: Plot plan with property lines, setbacks, easements, north arrow, and scale at minimum 1 inch = 20 feet
items.notes: Show adjacent structures, rights-of-way, alleys, and all existing improvements.

items.description: Accessible parking stall layout with dimensions, slope, and accessible route to building entrance
items.notes: Accessible van stall required for every 6 accessible spaces. Cross-slope max 2% in any direction.

items.description: Santa Rosa Planning Division Zoning Clearance (Form PD-100) attached to application
items.notes: Planning clearance required before Building Division acceptance.

items.description: WUI / Fire Zone 3 determination from SRFD, or documentation that property is outside Fire Zone 3
items.notes: Required for all new construction regardless of parcel size.

items.description: Automatic fire sprinkler system plans (NFPA 13 full system) or deferred submittal notation with SRFD pre-approval
items.notes: R-2 occupancies require NFPA 13 (not 13D) full fire sprinkler system.

items.description: Fire alarm system plans per NFPA 72 showing device locations, wiring, control panel, and annunciator panel
items.notes: R-2 buildings with more than 16 occupants require full fire alarm system with voice evacuation in common areas.

items.description: Fire-rated corridor and exit stair construction details including assembly rating, door/frame ratings, and hardware
items.notes: Corridors serving 10+ occupants require 1-hour rated construction. Stair enclosures per CBC-1023.

items.description: Occupancy separation details where R-2 is mixed with other occupancies (podium, parking, commercial)
items.notes: Required for mixed-occupancy buildings. Include separation table per CBC Table 508.4.

items.description: Complete structural framing plans and sections including gravity and lateral system
items.notes: Soft-story retrofit documentation required for pre-1980 concrete/masonry buildings per Santa Rosa local amendment.

items.description: Structural engineer of record wet stamp on all structural sheets
items.notes: Licensed California PE or SE required for R-2 buildings of any height.

items.description: Special inspection program (SIP) listing all required special inspections and inspector qualifications
items.notes: Required for buildings over 3 stories or using engineered wood products, HSS columns, or post-tensioned slabs.

items.description: Accessible unit matrix showing compliant Type A (fully accessible) and Type B (adaptable) unit counts and locations
items.notes: Type A: 10% of ground-floor units or all elevator-served units. Type B: all multi-family units on accessible routes.

items.description: Type A unit plans at 1/4-inch scale showing turning spaces, reach ranges, accessible kitchen, and bathroom layouts
items.notes: 60-inch turning circle in bathrooms. 36-inch minimum clear floor space at all appliances.

items.description: Common area accessibility plans including lobbies, mailrooms, laundry rooms, and recreation areas
items.notes: All common-use areas must be on accessible route. Include elevator cab dimensions where provided.

items.description: Mechanical plans and equipment schedule for all units and common areas
items.notes: All-electric HVAC preferred per Santa Rosa CARP. Unit-by-unit and common-area systems to be shown.

items.description: Kitchen and bathroom exhaust fan schedule with CFM rates and duct routing to exterior
items.notes: Per ASHRAE 62.2, bathroom fans: 50 CFM continuous or 110 CFM intermittent. Kitchen: 200 CFM intermittent.

items.description: Site electrical plan showing main service, distribution panels, EV charging provisions, and exterior lighting
items.notes: EV charging infrastructure required for all multi-family parking per Title 24 Part 6 and Santa Rosa CARP.

items.description: Panel schedules for main distribution panel and all unit sub-panels
items.notes: Show reserved capacity for future all-electric appliances per Santa Rosa local amendment.

items.description: CF-1R Certificate of Compliance for multi-family residential construction (HERS registered)
items.notes: Separate CF-1R may be required per unit type (floor plan type A, B, C, etc.).

items.description: Energy efficiency measures checklist including insulation, windows, water heating, and lighting power density
items.notes: Photovoltaic (solar) system required for new R-2 buildings per Title 24 Part 6 Section 150.1(c).

items.description: CALGreen Tier 1 mandatory checklist for multi-family residential (Santa Rosa requirement)
items.notes: Tier 1 mandatory for all new multi-family construction in Santa Rosa.

items.description: Construction Waste Management Plan with minimum two local C&D recycling facilities identified
items.notes: 65% minimum diversion required. Post-construction Diversion Report required before final.
